evals.prompt: I want to add a multi-tenant billing system to our SaaS app. It needs subscription plans, usage-based billing, Stripe integration, invoice generation, and an admin billing dashboard. Set up the spec-driven development workflow for this feature.
evals.expected_output: Spec directory created, requirements.md template with user stories, design.md template with API/schema, tasks.md with ordered implementation tasks, phase gates defined

assertions.name: spec_directory
assertions.description: Creates .claude/specs/billing/ or similar spec directory structure

assertions.name: requirements_template
assertions.description: requirements.md with user stories, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries (in/out)

assertions.name: design_template
assertions.description: design.md covering API endpoints, data model, and error handling

assertions.name: tasks_template
assertions.description: tasks.md with ordered tasks, dependencies, verification commands

assertions.name: phase_gates
assertions.description: Phase gates defined (approval required before advancing)

assertions.name: enforcement_rules
assertions.description: Enforcement rules stated (no code before design, no implementation before tasks)

evals.prompt: Set up SDD for adding team collaboration features. Needs: team creation with email invites, role-based access (owner, admin, member, guest), shared workspaces per team, activity feed showing member actions, and a team settings page. NestJS + React stack.
evals.expected_output: requirements.md with user stories per role, design.md with API/schema for teams and workspaces, tasks.md with ordered tasks, phase gates between each phase

assertions.name: spec_directory
assertions.description: Creates spec directory structure for the feature

assertions.name: requirements_with_roles
assertions.description: User stories for each role (owner, admin, member, guest) with different permissions

assertions.name: design_with_api
assertions.description: API endpoints for teams, invites, workspaces, and activity feed

assertions.name: tasks_with_dependencies
assertions.description: Tasks ordered with explicit dependencies (e.g., team model before invites)

assertions.name: phase_gates
assertions.description: Approval gates between requirements, design, tasks, and implementation

assertions.name: scope_boundaries
assertions.description: Explicit in-scope and out-of-scope lists

evals.prompt: Set up the spec-driven development workflow for building a full-text search system. We need to search across products, users, and orders using Elasticsearch. Features: indexing pipeline syncing from PostgreSQL, search API with filters and facets, autocomplete endpoint, and a search analytics dashboard. NestJS backend.
evals.expected_output: requirements.md with search-specific acceptance criteria, design.md covering Elasticsearch architecture and sync pipeline, tasks.md with ordered tasks

assertions.name: spec_directory
assertions.description: Creates spec directory structure

assertions.name: requirements_with_criteria
assertions.description: Acceptance criteria include search relevance, response time, and index freshness

assertions.name: design_covers_elasticsearch
assertions.description: Design covers index mappings, sync strategy (CDC vs polling), and query architecture

assertions.name: tasks_ordered
assertions.description: Tasks ordered: index setup before search API, search API before analytics

assertions.name: phase_gates
assertions.description: Phase gates requiring approval at each transition

assertions.name: enforcement_rules
assertions.description: Rules stated: no implementation before tasks are approved

evals.prompt: I want to build a webhook delivery system using SDD. Features: register webhook URLs per tenant, event type subscriptions, delivery with exponential backoff retry (max 5 attempts), HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, delivery logs with success/failure status, and a management UI showing recent deliveries. NestJS backend.
evals.expected_output: Three spec files with webhook-specific requirements (retry logic, signatures), design with delivery pipeline, tasks with verification commands

assertions.name: spec_directory
assertions.description: Creates spec directory with requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md

assertions.name: requirements_cover_retry
assertions.description: Requirements specify retry behavior (exponential backoff, max attempts, dead letter)

assertions.name: design_with_delivery_pipeline
assertions.description: Design covers the delivery pipeline: event -> queue -> deliver -> retry -> log

assertions.name: tasks_with_verification
assertions.description: Each task has a verification command to confirm it works

assertions.name: phase_gates
assertions.description: Approval required before advancing phases

assertions.name: enforcement_rules
assertions.description: No code before design review, no implementation before task approval

evals.prompt: Set up SDD for adding audit logging across our 5 NestJS microservices. Every data mutation (create, update, delete) on key entities should be logged with: who (userId), what (entity, field changes), when (timestamp), before/after values, and IP address. Logs go to a central audit-service backed by Elasticsearch for search and compliance reporting.
evals.expected_output: Requirements covering all 5 services, design with cross-service audit architecture, tasks broken down per service with integration tasks

assertions.name: spec_directory
assertions.description: Creates spec directory structure

assertions.name: requirements_cover_all_services
assertions.description: Requirements mention all 5 microservices and which entities are audited in each

assertions.name: design_cross_service
assertions.description: Design covers how audit events flow from each service to the central audit-service

assertions.name: tasks_per_service
assertions.description: Tasks broken down per service with a shared audit library/middleware task first

assertions.name: phase_gates
assertions.description: Phase gates defined between requirements, design, tasks, implementation

assertions.name: enforcement_rules
assertions.description: Enforcement rules present (no code before design, each task is one commit)
